Test Description: Verify GPIO register default reset values and masked write/read behavior across the defined register set using per-register read/write masks and skip lists. Writes six data patterns, reads back with masks, and compares against expected values computed from masks and defaults.
Remarks: - VRRW registers skipped per skip_array comment; - Default read masks applied clear bit0 (0xfffffffe) in reset-value check; - Note in code: 'when reading default values the din value is becoming 1 automatically if we don't force any value, but if we force zero to din bit level sel becoming high, so that reading value not matched with expected value'; - soft_reset_chk present but disabled (#ifdef 0).
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) Enter test_case: call chk_rst_val(); 2) chk_rst_val: for i=0..(CNT-1)=48: set addr = addr_array[i]; if skip_rst_array[i]==1: continue; if read_mask_array[i]==0x00000000: continue; read data_rd = read_reg(addr); compute data = (data_rd & 0xfffffffe); if data == default_value_array[i]: pass; else: def_fail_cnt++ and printf failure with addr, expected, read; 3) Return to test_case: call chk_rd_wr(); 4) chk_rd_wr: Initialize chk_val[6] = {0xffffffff,0xaaaaaaaa,0x55555555,0xf5f5f5f5,0xA5A5A5A5,0xffff0000}; For each pattern j=0..5: set data_wr = chk_val[j]; 4a) Write phase: for i=0..48: addr = addr_array[i]; if skip_array[i]==1: continue; if write_mask_array[i]==0x00000000: continue; write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i])); 4b) Read/verify phase: for i=0..48: addr = addr_array[i]; if skip_array[i]==1: continue; if write_mask_array[i]==0x00000000: continue; if read_mask_array[i]==0x00000000: continue; read masked data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]); compute wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff); compute exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])); if (data_rd == exp_val): pass; else: wr_fail_cnt++ and printf mismatch with addr, expected, read; 5) After loops: if (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0): finish(1); else: finish(0).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: - Default-value check passes for all readable, non-skipped registers: (data_rd & 0xfffffffe) == default_value_array[i]; - For each write pattern and each non-skipped, R/W register: (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]) equals exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask & write_mask) | (~write_mask & read_mask & default_value)); - Overall pass if def_fail_cnt==0 and wr_fail_cnt==0 at end and finish(0) called; any mismatch increments failure count and finish(1) indicates failure.
Gap Analysis: - soft_reset_chk path is disabled (#ifdef 0); - Several registers are excluded via skip_array/skip_rst_array (e.g., VRRW and DIN/IO CTRL groups) so they are not covered; - No negative tests for illegal writes beyond mask-based skipping; - No error handling for read_reg/write_reg failures beyond counters.

Test Description: Exercise per-pin negative-edge interrupts for GPIO[8..39]: configure each pin, enable one pin at a time, generate a falling edge via 0xA0243ffc toggling, wait for ISR to clear int_pend, verify group status bit set and cleared, and clear system/GIC status.
Remarks: - Behavior conditional on GPIO0/GPIO1 macros to select IRQ ID (87/88) and sysreg bits; - Uses external int_pend flag for ISR synchronization; - Drives pad control via 0xA0243ffc to generate edges.
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) Initialize: test_err=0; If GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87) and write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR); If GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88) and write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR); 2) Initialize drive: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff); 3) Configure per-pin regs: for i=0..31: addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4); write_reg(addr1, (1u<<20)|(1u<<18)|(1u<<16)); wait_on(10); 4) For each pin i=0..31: wr_val = (1u<<i); 4a) Clear raw status: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val); 4b) Enable only this pin interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val); wait_on(10); 4c) Arm and generate negedge: set int_pend=1; write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff); wait_on(30); write_reg(0xA0243ffc, ~wr_val); 4d) Poll for ISR completion with timeout: timeout=5000; while(int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10); on timeout==0: printf timeout error and test_err++; 5) On completion: finish(test_err). ISR (Default_IRQHandler) runtime flow: A) local_wr=(1u<<i); set int_pend=0; write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff); B) Read pin reg raddr = base + i*4; rdata = read_reg(raddr); if ((rdata & 0x1)!=0) test_err++; C) If ((rdata & 0x2)!=0x0): read rdata_grp=read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if ((rdata_grp & local_wr)==0) test_err++; write_reg(raddr,(1u<<20)|(1u<<16)); write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr); read rdata_grp again; if (rdata_grp!=0x0) test_err++; If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87); If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88); Else branch (if (rdata&0x2)==0): test_err++.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: - No per-pin timeout during polling (int_pend cleared within 5000 iterations with wait_on(10) each); - In ISR: (rdata & 0x1)==0; (rdata & 0x2)!=0x0; (read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 & (1u<<i)) != 0; After clears, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 == 0x0; System raw status cleared for selected GPIOx; Final finish(test_err) equals 0.
Gap Analysis: - No debounce/noise filtering or repeated-edge robustness checks; - Relies on 0xA0243ffc external drive without verifying actual pin DIN; - No negative tests for disabled interrupts or spurious IRQs; - Limited error handling (increments counter only).

Test Description: Enable posedge interrupts on all GPIO[8..39] pins, drive a rising edge per iteration, wait for ISR to acknowledge and clear, verify group status behavior, clear per-pin raw and system status, and re-enable for next iteration.
Remarks: - Behavior conditional on GPIO0/GPIO1 macros (IRQ IDs 87/88 and sysreg bits); - Uses volatile extern int_pend for ISR synchronization; - Configures IO control groups 1..4 to 0x000000FF before testing.
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) If GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87); If GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88); 2) Enable sysreg interrupt: If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR); If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR); 3) Configure per-pin posedge: for i=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), 0x00020000); wait_on(10); 4) Put GPIOs 8-39 in input mode: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1,0x000000FF); write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2,0x000000FF); write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3,0x000000FF); write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4,0x000000FF); wait_on(10); 5) Enable group interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF); 6) For each i=0..31: drive low then rising edge and wait for ISR: write_reg(0xA0243ffc,0x00000000); wait_on(10); set int_pend=1; write_reg(0xA0243ffc,0xFFFFFFFF); Poll with timeout=2000: while(int_pend==1 && --timeout>0) wait_on(10); if timeout==0: printf timeout error and test_err++; write_reg(0xA0243ffc,0x00000000); wait_on(10); 7) finish(test_err). ISR (Default_IRQHandler): A) wr_val=(1<<i); set int_pend=0; B) Read group status rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); mask group: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1,0x00000000); if ((rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF)!=0): success log; else: error and test_err++; C) Clear per-pin raw for all pins: for j=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j*4), 0x00010000); wait_on(2); D) Verify clear: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp!=0x0): error and test_err++; E) Clear system raw: If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR) and read back to ensure bit cleared; If GPIO1: same with GPIO1; if not cleared: error and test_err++; F) Re-enable group: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1,0xFFFFFFFF); G) Clear GIC IRQ: GIC_ClearIRQ(87/88).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: - No per-iteration timeout (int_pend cleared before timeout of ~2000 loops with wait_on(10)); - ISR observes non-zero group status on entry; - After clearing per-pin raw, group status reads 0x0; - System RAW_STCR1 bit cleared upon write/readback; - Final finish(test_err)==0.
Gap Analysis: - Does not validate per-pin DIN values directly; - No negative tests for disabled interrupts or noise; - Relies on shared drive register 0xA0243ffc; - Limited error handling beyond counters.
